pattern: Local-maximum scan: check each interior element against both immediate neighbors in one linear pass, since a peak can only be judged by what's directly beside it.
hints: The first and last positions can never count, no matter their values — only interior positions are candidates.
For every interior index, compare it only to its immediate left and right neighbor; nothing farther away matters.
An index only qualifies when it is strictly greater than both neighbors — equal heights on either side disqualify it, so a plateau never produces a peak.

edge_inputs.description: all values identical, no interior element stands out
single interior peak surrounded by lower values
two separate peaks with a valley between them
plateau at the candidate peak equals one neighbor, so it must not count
strictly increasing sequence, last element excluded so no peak exists
strictly decreasing sequence, first element excluded so no peak exists
minimum allowed length with a peak in the middle
maximum allowed length, alternating high/low values at max magnitude
